title: Define open module participation and workflow obligations
lane_outcome: Define a reviewed open module participation, workflow obligation, and task-posture model for #1392 so #1389 simplification does not hard-code today's first-party modules, fixed workflow slots, or static AGENTS.md instructions.
purpose_for_parent: This is the prerequisite lane for #1389. It establishes how the recommended ordinary loop composes with repo workflow obligations, arbitrary module contributions, and task-specific posture/dynamic instruction routing before the visible-surface classification in #1390 and per-step simplification in #1391.
subsystems: task posture and dynamic instruction routing
technical_strategy: Keep the first slice design-shaped. Inspect current contract, report, docs, and first-party module evidence; define the minimal participation vocabulary and optional extension points; map Planning, Memory, and Verification as examples; then route concrete contract/schema/doc changes into follow-on slices instead of changing internals prematurely.
technology_choices: Use checked-in issue-linked Planning as the durable owner for lane sequencing.
Use reviewed design evidence before product-source mutations.
Keep module participation generic; first-party modules are evidence, not the whole model.

slice_sequence.title: Define participation vocabulary and evidence map
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Produce the reviewed design basis required by #1392: minimal participation contract, workflow-obligation composition, module contributions, conflict surfacing, startup exposure, and first-party module mapping.
slice_sequence.proof: Implemented contract/schema/runtime/docs/tests expose and validate participation_model; evidence cites module_registry, schema reference docs, docs/package/modules.md, docs/package/installed-surfaces.md, and Planning/Memory/Verification README surfaces.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: The initial open participation vocabulary is complete and extended by the task-posture/dynamic-instruction slice in this PR.

slice_sequence.title: Route contract, schema, and docs follow-ups
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Turn accepted design gaps into bounded implementation issues or execplans with exact touched surfaces and proof.
slice_sequence.proof: Each follow-up has an owner surface, changed-path proof expectation, and explicit dependency impact on #1390/#1391.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: The lane may close once #1390 and #1391 have enough criteria to proceed without re-litigating the participation model.

slice_sequence.title: Define task posture and dynamic instruction routing
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Answer the sharpened #1392 scope: AW as a richer configurable AGENTS.md that keeps the static adapter small and emits task-specific posture, obligations, skill routes, allowed/forbidden actions, proof/closeout boundaries, and module contributions only when relevant.
slice_sequence.proof: Reviewed contract or implementation evidence covers task/operating posture packets, module contributions to posture/output/review/authority boundaries, composition with optimization/artifact/initiative/assurance/read-budget posture, startup projection rules, and closeout/report adherence visibility.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: This PR now covers the task-posture/dynamic-instruction model at contract level. Deeper runtime implementation of every projection may still be split later, but #1392 has a model-level answer.

slice_sequence.title: Emit compact task posture packets at runtime
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Implement the runtime behavior named by the #1392 model: start, implement --changed, and relevant report/summary paths emit a compact task_posture_packet only when task facts, config posture, workflow obligations, or module participation change routing. Owner surface: src/agentic_workspace/cli.py, src/agentic_workspace/workspace_runtime_primitives.py, startup/implement/report packet builders, task posture schema references, and focused CLI tests.
slice_sequence.proof: Implemented runtime packet builder and focused tests for start, implement --changed, summary --select task_posture_packet, and report packet visibility.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: Runtime outputs now include task_posture_packet when posture changes allowed action, proof burden, skill route, authority boundary, output posture, read budget, or closeout expectations. Later #1389 simplification slices may tune presentation, but emission is no longer only a named architecture.

slice_sequence.title: Keep task posture packet schema shape synchronized
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Remove or guard the drift risk from copying the same task_posture_packet shape into startup, implementer, and workspace report schemas. Owner surface: src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/startup_context.schema.json, src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/implementer_context.schema.json, src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/workspace_report.schema.json, schema generation/reference tooling, and contract tooling checks.
slice_sequence.proof: Startup, implementer, and workspace report schemas now require the same task_posture_packet core, including dynamic_instruction_projection and posture_adherence fields.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: The copied schemas are synchronized for this PR. A later generated-schema extraction may reduce duplication, but #1392 no longer leaves the shared packet shape unsynchronized.

slice_sequence.title: Show posture adherence in closeout and reports
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Make posture failures visible as their own closeout/report concern, not only as skipped workflow steps. Owner surface: workspace report builders, Planning closeout/report projections, proof/intent/residue closeout fields, and tests that exercise complied/diverged/gap states.
slice_sequence.proof: Runtime task_posture_packet includes posture_adherence, report includes task_posture_packet, and closeout gate tests block full completion while posture follow-through slices remain planned.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: Closeout/report can name the applied posture and ask for complied/diverged/gap review. Later #1389 reductions may simplify presentation, but adherence accounting is durable.

slice_sequence.title: Own dynamic AGENTS.md projection semantics
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Turn the static-adapter boundary into an implementation path: where task-specific instruction projection is emitted, what fields are rendered, and how provenance and authority boundaries are preserved. Owner surface: AGENTS.md adapter guidance, startup dynamic instruction projection payloads, skill routing/startup selectors, module participation declarations, and docs/package/installed-surfaces.md.
slice_sequence.proof: task_posture_packet.dynamic_instruction_projection is emitted by start, summary, implement, and report packet paths while AGENTS.md remains the static adapter pointer.
slice_sequence.residual_after_slice: The static adapter remains small and runtime projection carries task-specific posture, obligations, skills, allowed/forbidden actions, proof/closeout boundaries, output posture, and provenance. #1390/#1391 may still reduce presentation or command overlap.

acceptance_boundary: This lane may be called complete only when #1392 has a reviewed open participation and task-posture model that answers its current acceptance questions, names follow-on contract/schema/doc criteria, and gives #1390/#1391 enough guidance to classify and simplify current surfaces without re-litigating architecture.
proof_strategy: Aggregate design-review evidence against #1392 acceptance first. Implementation proof is required only for follow-on contract/schema/doc slices that actually change product surfaces; lane proof must record any known gaps before closeout.

evidence: docs/package/modules.md and docs/package/installed-surfaces.md document the open participation boundary.
packages/planning/README.md, packages/memory/README.md, and packages/verification/README.md map first-party module examples.
tests/test_workspace_modules_cli.py asserts the modules CLI participation payload.
participation_model.task_posture_model defines the task posture packet, composition inputs, fields, module rule, and boundaries.
participation_model.dynamic_instruction_projection defines static AGENTS.md boundary, selection inputs, projection outputs, and adherence provenance.
modules[*].participation declares Planning, Memory, and Verification posture triggers, dynamic projections, authority boundaries, and conflict provenance.
src/agentic_workspace/workspace_runtime_primitives.py emits task_posture_packet from start, implement, summary, and report paths.
startup, implementer, and workspace report schemas require dynamic_instruction_projection and posture_adherence in task_posture_packet.
Closeout completion gate blocks full-intent completion while task-posture follow-through slices remain planned.
tests/test_workspace_start_preflight_cli.py covers default start and summary task_posture_packet emission.
tests/test_workspace_report_cli.py covers the task-posture closeout blocker.
make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, make maintainer-surfaces, and make schema-reference-docs passed after closeout.
known_gaps: Per-module participation fields are not yet added to every module descriptor.
Workflow obligation reports do not yet carry stronger structured module/slot provenance.
Module reports remain weakly structured until a later simplification slice needs stricter fields.
Workflow obligation reports may still need stronger structured provenance if #1390/#1391 require machine-checkable conflict tables.

residual_lane_work: No direct #1392 runtime posture follow-up remains planned in this lane. Downstream #1390/#1391/#1389 work may still tune presentation, provenance depth, or simplification around these surfaces.
lane_to_epic_contribution: Defines the open operating-substrate, task-posture, and dynamic-instruction boundary that the rest of #1389 must preserve while simplifying ordinary agent-facing workflow.

closeout_state.summary: #1392 implemented: open module participation, task posture, runtime packet emission, shared packet schema fields, closeout/report posture visibility, and dynamic instruction projection are covered in this PR.
closeout_state.residual_work: #1390 ordinary-loop classification, #1391 per-step simplification, and future #1389 concrete reductions remain separate downstream work.

references.label: open module participation and workflow obligations model
ordinary continuity loop classification follows this lane

notes: Priority: first lane under #1389 because #1392 explicitly precedes #1390 and #1391. This PR now includes the task-posture and dynamic-instruction model requested by PR review; do not claim the full #1389 lane complete.
